decisions.relationship_evidence: Retrospectively compares the CDC hospital-onset Adult Sepsis Event definition with reportable HAI conditions and quantifies infections missed by each approach.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of the coverage and clinical significance of an alternative surveillance definition.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly proposes snowball sampling for early-outbreak serosurveys and evaluates its efficiency through examples and simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Method-development paper proposing and demonstrating a surveillance sampling design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates prognostic biomarkers for fatal SFTS outcomes, not a surveillance-measurement design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for surveillance measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulation directly benchmarks border-arrival, hospital, and community sampling strategies by time to detection of novel variants.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Performance comparison of alternative surveillance designs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes clinical phenotyping of pulmonary long COVID rather than an infectious-disease surveillance design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the assigned surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulation to evaluate event-entry screening with rapid antigen tests, including detection, predictive values, and cost per case.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of a screening-measurement design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents remote clinical monitoring architecture for individual COVID-19 patients, not epidemiologic surveillance measurement.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates the novel Oxford Benchmark Scale for Rating Vaccine Technologies using exploratory and confirmatory factor analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit measurement-scale development and validation source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses standardized CLABSI surveillance protocols, case forms, denominator collection, and centralized data-quality review across an Indian hospital network.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multicentre infectious-disease surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a six-step, data-driven model for selecting pathogens for acute-febrile-illness surveillance and applies it to customize a multiplex panel.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a surveillance selection tool.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts multi-pathogen surveillance of febrile patients using a 25-pathogen TaqMan Array Card and analyzes detected coinfections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-disease application of multi-pathogen surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates SPIN-UTI surveillance data against retrospective clinical-record review and estimates sensitivity, specificity, PPV, and NPV.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of infection-surveillance measurements.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares self-reported receipt of eight adult vaccines with electronic medical records and estimates standard validity measures and reporting bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of self-reported vaccination measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian latent-class analysis to compare herdsmen reports with serologic tests and estimate reporting sensitivity and specificity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of self-reported disease measurement against serology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates laboratory-date proxies against encounter-based retention using agreement, discrimination, sensitivity, specificity, and misclassification error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of a proxy surveillance measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates parental vaccination recall against card data using agreement, bias, sensitivity, and specificity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of recall-based coverage measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares routine ICD- and laboratory-based surveillance with standardized prospective screening and PCR, quantifying missed infections and case-definition sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct diagnostic-accuracy assessment of respiratory-infection surveillance methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates maternal recall against vaccination records and estimates agreement, recall bias, sensitivity, and specificity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of recall-based vaccination measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates householders' and village headmen's FMD reports against serological findings, including sensitivity and specificity estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of verbal disease reports for surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses maternal vaccination recall against home-based cards using agreement, sensitivity, specificity, and recall bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of maternal-recall measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates concordance of self-reported smoking with plasma cotinine and reports sensitivity and specificity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct biomarker validation of self-reported behavior.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates survey-reported adult vaccination against EHR and immunization-system data, estimating sensitivity, specificity, and net bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of self-reported vaccination status.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes implementation of complementary CDC systems for facility benchmarking, national estimation, and sentinel monitoring of inpatient antimicrobial use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit implementation source for a structured monitoring strategy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes a multistage pediatric enteric-disease platform spanning clinical recruitment, specimen collection, molecular testing, follow-up, and economic modeling.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete implementation of a structured infectious-disease study platform.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares blood-culture capacity and utilization at two hospitals; sentinel surveillance is only recommended, not implemented or evaluated as a platform.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No concrete structured surveillance platform is established.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes the structure and country-tailored activities of the Latin American Pertussis Project, including surveillance evaluation, laboratory quality assurance, and reporting support.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit implementation account of a multinational surveillance-strengthening platform.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes systematically collected data from a longstanding community-based HIV testing network to monitor testing and linkage outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of a structured HIV monitoring network.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a hospital-based enhanced surveillance system at two pediatric hospitals to detect and characterize infant pertussis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-disease application of enhanced surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses serologic testing of free-roaming dogs as environmental sentinels for population exposure to Toxoplasma gondii.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete zoonotic sentinel-surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Designs and implements two complementary blood-donor bioarchives for targeted and continuous pathogen surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit implementation of structured bioarchive surveillance systems.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Specifies and initiates three prospective cohorts with molecular testing and comparison to national laboratory and syndromic surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Protocol and implementation account for a structured infectious-intestinal-disease platform.
named_source_mentions: Second Study of Infectious Intestinal Disease (IID2 study)

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews infection-control surveillance systems across post-Soviet Bloc countries and identifies underreporting, weak infrastructure, and reform needs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Review-based guidance on strengthening infection-control surveillance systems.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly examines Australian immunization registers, identifying access, underreporting, timeliness, and linkage limitations and improvement opportunities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: System-focused review offering explicit register-improvement guidance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews measurement problems in maternal-sepsis mortality, explicitly identifying underreporting and misclassification in routine registration data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Review guidance concerning weaknesses in mortality measurement and reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Provides post-exposure-prophylaxis recommendations; injury underreporting is contextual rather than the evaluated or developed method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for surveillance-system design or measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Provides clinical guidance on hepatitis B protection and postexposure management, not surveillance-system design or reporting methodology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the exact surveillance concept.
named_source_mentions: Immunization of health-care personnel: recommendations of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices

decisions.relationship_evidence: Identifies limitations of fragmented animal, pathogen, and foodborne-disease surveillance and specifies integrated approaches to improve coverage, quality, and speed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit guidance on integrated surveillance-system development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes a new approach that adds a brief behavioral questionnaire to routine HIV sentinel surveillance, piloted and subsequently rolled out nationally.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological introduction of an enhanced sentinel-surveillance design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Maps clinical STI service delivery and patient flow at facilities; it does not develop or evaluate the sentinel surveillance method itself.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Sentinel-site assignment is only the setting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly introduces the concept of surveillance fatigue to characterize epidemic underreporting and illustrates it using past epidemics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concept-development source for a reporting phenomenon in epidemic surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates feasibility, sensitivity, local discrimination, turnaround, and cost of lot quality assurance sampling for population-based AMR surveys.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct performance evaluation of an alternative surveillance sampling design.
named_source_mentions: 68th World Health Assembly Global Action Plan on AMR

decisions.relationship_evidence: Shows that short follow-up and inconsistent wound-event definitions can materially understate groin complication rates and calls for uniform reporting standards.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Evidence-based guidance on reporting definitions and follow-up windows.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies implant retrieval analysis to prosthetic-joint infection timing; surveillance-system design is not developed or evaluated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the exact surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveys national RSV surveillance structures, laboratory practices, and outputs across EU/EEA countries and identifies comparability and underreporting problems.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct cross-system evaluation of surveillance practices.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes weaknesses in emerging aquatic-animal disease detection and reporting and recommends farmer-centered, technological, and international reporting reforms.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance and reporting-improvement guidance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses expert surveys to assess feasibility and barriers of national SSI quality measures and identifies methodological and data-system requirements.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical assessment of a proposed surveillance measurement framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews EU Legionnaires' disease surveillance definitions, responsibilities, methods, and outputs while identifying underreporting and underdiagnosis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Methodological overview and improvement guidance for a multinational surveillance system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Discusses the aims and challenges of epidemic nowcasting but does not explicitly originate, apply, or evaluate the assigned broad surveillance-system concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role established by the abstract.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly documents barriers in Asia-Pacific SSI surveillance and recommends standardized definitions, methods, accountability, training, and national programs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Expert guidance for designing and strengthening SSI surveillance programs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes risk-based case-finding and sentinel-surveillance systems, including indicators and a spreadsheet model for national prevalence estimation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development source for adaptable surveillance methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops two organizational models from interviews and network mapping to explain differences in passive-surveillance reporting performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical diagnosis of organizational determinants of surveillance reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly proposes a framework for designing and conducting descriptive epidemiologic studies and details its required measurement elements and biases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Method-development source for an epidemiologic measurement framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a new integrated framework for active surveillance of antiretroviral safety in pregnancy, including harmonized outcomes, linked data, and implementation targets.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-framework development source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes concrete use of influenza sentinel, laboratory, preparedness, and population-study systems for COVID-19 surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Application of established surveillance systems to SARS-CoV-2.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Advocates collection of gender-identity data in clinical trials but does not address infectious-disease surveillance-system or reporting methodology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the assigned concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Discusses selective clinical microbiology result reporting for treatment decisions, not public-health surveillance reporting design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explains shortcomings in EU/EEA surveillance focus, vigilance, and resilience and the specific system changes intended to address them.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Authoritative explanatory guidance for a long-term surveillance framework.
named_source_mentions: long-term surveillance framework 2021-2027 for infectious diseases in the European Union/European Economic Area

decisions.relationship_evidence: Summarizes data sources for studying fungal-disease disparities; underdiagnosis and underreporting are interpretive limitations rather than an evaluated surveillance method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact surveillance-system lineage role.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Provides clinical diagnostic-testing recommendations for respiratory infections, not a surveillance-system design or reporting framework.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes 20 years of Romanian legislation governing HAI definitions, reporting, and surveillance structures and identifies implementation barriers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of the evolution and weaknesses of a surveillance-reporting framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a conceptual risk-management framework describing the evolution of mpox preparedness, surveillance, mandatory reporting, response, and routine management.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit framework-development source grounded in national infectious-disease practice.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a modified Delphi process to identify adult RSV surveillance limitations and recommend integrated systems, standardized definitions, year-round testing, and adjustment for underestimation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit expert guidance for RSV surveillance design and measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses system dynamics and stakeholder-validated causal-loop diagrams to diagnose surveillance, funding, governance, and reporting feedback loops in Kenyan rabies control.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical systems diagnosis identifying surveillance-policy intervention points.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops clinical prehospital sepsis recognition and treatment guidance; suspected-case underreporting is motivation rather than a surveillance method under study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for surveillance-system design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements a needs assessment and training intervention addressing underreported needlestick injuries and institutional occupational-health capacity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete implementation of a reporting and capacity-improvement intervention.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes mandatory-notification data after diagnostic, regulatory, and guideline changes intended to strengthen Legionnaires' disease surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Evaluation of surveillance performance following reporting-system improvements.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Qualitatively investigates deliberate HAI underreporting mechanisms, their punitive incentives, and proposed prevention strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct diagnosis of behavioral and institutional causes of reporting failure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Critiques subjective HAI surveillance as biased and unreliable and argues for objective definitions and automated infection determination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Methodological rationale guiding surveillance automation and reporting improvement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses focus groups and interviews to identify consumer and clinician barriers to foodborne-illness diagnosis and reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct empirical diagnosis of reporting barriers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses qualitative interviews to identify barriers and motivations governing abortion reporting in clinical brucellosis surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct diagnosis of factors reducing surveillance sensitivity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveys family physicians to identify knowledge, motivation, and system factors responsible for infectious-disease underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical diagnosis of reporting failure and improvement opportunities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes field efforts and concrete solutions intended to improve childhood-TB detection, program integration, management, and reporting in Pakistan.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Field implementation account of detection and reporting improvements.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies the item-count technique to estimate concealed FMD reporting behavior and examines its relationship with farmer knowledge.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of behavioral causes of underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Cross-checks survey and registry records to quantify extreme under-notification of HPV doses and identifies software and school catch-up improvements.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of reporting completeness and improvement opportunities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Links laboratory and national surveillance records to quantify chlamydia underreporting and missing fields, then specifies a revised laboratory-reporting design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct reporting-quality evaluation supporting system redesign.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly reviews impediments and consequences of dengue underreporting and proposes remedies across clinical and community reporting levels.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit guidance for addressing infectious-disease underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents an evidence-based national STI strategy that explicitly proposes active surveillance, active case finding, and routine coordination with clinics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: National strategic guidance for a redesigned STI surveillance system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Maps TB diagnostic capacity and use, identifies spatial and guideline-adherence gaps, and evaluates determinants of test underuse linked to missed cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct diagnosis of case-detection system gaps relevant to reporting improvement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes centralizing multiple Ebola data sources and strengthening surveillance and social mobilization where actual and reported deaths diverged.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete outbreak surveillance-improvement implementation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Pilots a FHIR-based application that automatically retrieves EHR data and generates electronic STI case reports.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software implementation for automated electronic case reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements combined SSI surveillance linking several health databases with targeted record review to recover post-discharge cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete implementation of a sustainable reporting-completeness method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveys pediatricians' mumps testing, reporting, and outbreak-response knowledge and documents practices likely to cause underdiagnosis and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct diagnosis of clinician-level reporting gaps.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Identifies surveillance and diagnostic deficiencies for ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis and recommends active clinical, vector, and electronic surveillance improvements.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Expert subcommittee guidance for surveillance-system improvement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Constructs a clinical fever-outpatient management model; absence of underreporting is an outcome, not evidence of a surveillance reporting method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and pilots a modified VAP surveillance definition adapted to resource-constrained Indian hospitals, with standardized forms and validation infrastructure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and testing source for an adapted surveillance protocol.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks surgeon-led and infection-control-team SSI surveillance against a one-year, multi-source reference method and quantifies missed cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct comparative evaluation of surveillance sensitivity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveys Iranian nurses to quantify needlestick injuries, reporting completeness, and barriers to reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct reporting-quality and barrier evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates first-year mandatory MRSA bloodstream-infection notifications, identifies misrouted reports, and recommends reporting and risk-data improvements.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of a mandatory notification system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares one-day prevalence estimates with national KISS surveillance outputs and investigates possible systematic underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical benchmark of surveillance-system output against standardized prevalence measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Quantifies unreported needlestick and sharps injuries and examines reporting barriers and procedural awareness by occupation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct reporting-completeness and barrier study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses surveys and interviews to measure operating-theatre injury reporting and identify factors producing underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of incident-reporting quality and determinants.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses time-series analysis to compare voluntary and mandatory HIV notification and measures changes in case detection and missing data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of reporting-policy performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Examines national PUE surveillance records to assess underuse, inconsistent reporting, and geographic surveillance bias in H7N9 epidemiology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of surveillance bias and reporting performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Outlines and demonstrates a framework using hospitalization records and surveillance-to-hospitalization ratios to evaluate reporting-system performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a surveillance-evaluation framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates Zimbabwe's notifiable-disease system using interviews, resource checks, and record review, assessing utility, acceptability, simplicity, and sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct surveillance-system evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses Massachusetts LTBI surveillance data and concludes that substantial under-ascertainment or underreporting persists despite mandatory reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Evaluation of reporting-system sufficiency and case ascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Measures hepatitis C reporting rate, timeliness, underreporting, false reporting, and diagnostic-report concordance across medical institutions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct reporting-quality evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveys availability and utilization of HIV laboratory technologies; incomplete survey reporting is a limitation, not the reporting system being evaluated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for surveillance-system evaluation or reporting quality.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates BCG immunization-program implementation; registry underreporting is acknowledged but not itself systematically validated or benchmarked.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit reporting-quality evaluation sufficient for lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares EWARS and EWARN case rates and reporting quality against WHO benchmarks, quantifying underreporting, completeness, and timeliness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct comparative evaluation of two infectious-disease monitoring systems.
